LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: PETITIONER ELIE SASSON'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FROM THE SUSPENSION OF PETITIONERS CALIFORNIA DRIVERS LICENSE IS DENIED. SEE WORD (.DOC) MINUTES. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE ADOPTED RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of Petitioners California Drivers License
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Rose Mishaan (Email: rose.mishaan@gmail.com) of the law offices of Rose Mishaan (Tel: 415.565.9600) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party

Deputy Attorney General, Michael P. Purcell (Email: michael.purcell@doj.ca.gov) of the California Department of Justice (Tel: 510.879.0188) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, Department of Motor Vehicles

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER ELIE SASSON's Petition For Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of Petitioners California Drivers License is DENIED. See full WORD (.doc) minutes.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
